text: MiDAS issued automated unemployment-fraud determinations that were later found overwhelmingly erroneous. Claimants were assessed penalties and collection actions after automated determinations without adequate process.
canonical_source_conflicted: Unemployment insurance fraud standards and constitutional due-process requirements
mitigation_gap: Automated flags lacked meaningful human review and adequate notice/opportunity to contest; error monitoring and escalation failed.
reliance_or_harm: Thousands of claimants were wrongfully accused and faced garnishments, penalties, and collection actions; state settled for $20 million.
